prompt: Write a Python script using Biopython and py3Dmol that: (1) downloads all AlphaFold2 predicted structures for the human kinase superfamily (534 kinases) from the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database, (2) parses each structure to extract the activation loop (DFG+1 to APE motifs), (3) computes per-residue pLDDT scores for the activation loop, (4) clusters kinases by activation loop conformation using hierarchical clustering on backbone dihedral angles, (5) outputs a CSV of {uniprot_id, kinase_family, mean_loop_pLDDT, cluster_id} and a heatmap figure using matplotlib. Use Python 3.10+, pandas, numpy, scipy, and Biopython. Include error handling for missing structures and progress logging.
source_citation: Derived from AlphaFold Protein Structure Database (v4); target: human kinome; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
